name: Winged rainbow unicorn
description: This Schleich Winged rainbow unicorn figure is a beautifully crafted magical creature featuring a spiralling horn and ethereal coat. Hand-painted with exquisite detail from the bayala® fantasy realm.
marketingDescription: Hidden in the mountains of Rainbow Island, there lives a Pegasus unicorn with its little one. Their wings light up in all colours of the rainbow, even more magnificently than any of the other creatures of this colourful island. The winged rainbow unicorn has recently begun taking along its little one on its wings across the island to the end of the rainbow. The elves there know and love them. Whenever they see them, they clap and cheer for them, and they look forward to the day of the Rainbow Festival. Because then the two of them come to the elves and they celebrate together. It is the most beautiful and colourful festival in all of BAYALA. Guests come together from all corners to bid welcome to the two wondrous, flying creatures and to behold their beauty and majesty of colour. Do you want to celebrate with them?

name: Winged rainbow unicorn foal
description: This Schleich Winged rainbow unicorn foal figure depicts a young foal with characteristic long legs and curious posture. Hand-painted with careful attention to detail, the figure captures the playful energy that makes foals so appealing.
marketingDescription: Hidden in the mountains of Rainbow Island, there lives a Pegasus unicorn with its little one. Their wings light up in all colours of the rainbow, even more magnificently than any of the other creatures of this colourful island. The winged rainbow unicorn has recently begun taking along its little one on its wings across the island to the end of the rainbow. The elves there know and love them. Whenever they see them, they clap and cheer for them, and they look forward to the day of the Rainbow Festival. Because then the two of them come to the elves and they celebrate together. It is the most beautiful and colourful festival in all of BAYALA®. Guests come together from all corners to bid welcome to the two wondrous, flying creatures and to behold their beauty and majesty of colour. Do you want to celebrate with them?

name: Horse Club Riding School
description: This Schleich Horse Club Riding School figure is a hand-painted replica capturing the beauty and grace of this horse with lifelike detail, from its coat colouring to its natural posture.

name: Haflinger Mare
description: This Schleich Haflinger Mare figure is a hand-painted replica of the Haflinger breed from the Austrian mountains, featuring the characteristic chestnut coat with flaxen mane and tail.

name: Haflinger stallion
description: This Schleich Haflinger stallion figure is a hand-painted replica of the Haflinger breed from the Austrian mountains, featuring the characteristic chestnut coat with flaxen mane and tail.

name: Haflinger Foal
description: This Schleich Haflinger Foal figure is a hand-painted replica of the Haflinger breed from the Austrian mountains, featuring the characteristic chestnut coat with flaxen mane and tail. As a foal, it captures the long-legged charm and playful energy of a young horse.

name: Icelandic Pony stallion
description: This Schleich Icelandic Pony stallion figure is a hand-painted replica of the Icelandic horse, a breed developed over a thousand years in isolation, capturing the sturdy build and distinctive character of these sure-footed horses.

name: Hanoverian mare
description: This Schleich Hanoverian mare figure is a hand-painted replica of the Hanoverian warmblood from northern Germany, showcasing the athletic build and powerful gaits that make this breed a champion in equestrian sport.

name: Hanoverian Foal
description: This Schleich Hanoverian Foal figure is a hand-painted replica of the Hanoverian warmblood from northern Germany, showcasing the athletic build and powerful gaits that make this breed a champion in equestrian sport. As a foal, it captures the long-legged charm and playful energy of a young horse.

name: Lipizzaner Mare
description: This Schleich Lipizzaner Mare figure is a hand-painted replica of the Lipizzaner breed, famous for the Spanish Riding School in Vienna, capturing the classical elegance and strength of these renowned dressage horses.
marketingDescription: Lipizzaners can master difficult dressage exercises with ease. The breed is best known for the capriole. This is a jump in place in which all four of the horses' hoofs are in the air. At the highest point, it kicks out with the hind legs before landing.

name: Lipizzaner Foal
description: This Schleich Lipizzaner Foal figure is a hand-painted replica of the Lipizzaner breed, famous for the Spanish Riding School in Vienna, capturing the classical elegance and strength of these renowned dressage horses. As a foal, it captures the long-legged charm and playful energy of a young horse.

name: Andalusian stallion
description: This Schleich Andalusian stallion figure is a hand-painted replica of the Andalusian breed, originating from the Iberian Peninsula, showcasing its elegant build and powerful yet graceful stance suited to classical dressage.
marketingDescription: Only Andalusians that are listed in the studbook can be designated a Pure Spanish Horse (PRE). In order to be listed, they have to undergo a registration and selection process. The horse is inspected and its gaits and physical build are assessed.

name: Andalusian foal
description: This Schleich Andalusian foal figure is a hand-painted replica of the Andalusian breed, originating from the Iberian Peninsula, showcasing its elegant build and powerful yet graceful stance suited to classical dressage. As a foal, it captures the long-legged charm and playful energy of a young horse.
marketingDescription: Andalusian foals aren't fully grown until the age of six or seven. This is significantly later than foals from other horse breeds. In the first few years, they are gently introduced to wearing a halter and being showered. This helps the foals to learn to trust people.

name: Duck
description: This Schleich Duck figure is a hand-painted bird replica, capturing the plumage, posture, and distinctive features with lifelike accuracy.

name: Drake
description: This Schleich Drake figure is a hand-painted farm animal replica with realistic proportions and distinctive features.
marketingDescription: Male ducks are easily identifiable by their colourful, iridescent plumage. After breeding, the drakes lose their colourful feathers and don their so-called eclipse plumage. During this time, only the yellow bill can be used to tell them apart from the females.

name: Pinto Mare
description: This Schleich Pinto Mare figure portrays a mare with graceful proportions and a calm, confident stance. Hand-painted to highlight the musculature and coat details, this figure reflects the quiet strength of a female horse.
marketingDescription: Pinto horses were much loved by American Indians, because their coloured coat served as great camouflage. In the summer they preferred dun horses, chestnuts and sorrels. In the winter they rode on white Pintos. They were difficult to make out against a snowy landscape.

name: Tinker stallion
description: This Schleich Tinker stallion figure is a hand-painted replica of the Tinker (Irish Cob), showcasing the sturdy, compact build, abundant feathering, and striking piebald or skewbald colouring of this traditional breed.
marketingDescription: Coloured cobs are powerful horses with a full mane, strong feathering, a short back and large hooves. The typical piebald coat pattern was deliberately bred into the horses around the year 1900. They were first called "Magpies". Cobs are very balanced and meek.

name: Tennessee Walker gelding
description: This Schleich Tennessee Walker gelding figure is a hand-painted replica capturing the beauty and grace of this horse with lifelike detail, from its coat colouring to its natural posture.
marketingDescription: If you look at a Tennessee Walker from the side, its legs do not stand straight on the ground, but are slanted. This is especially noticeable on the hind legs. The position of its legs enables it to master certain gaits - flat walk, running walk and canter.

name: Tennessee Walker mare
description: This Schleich Tennessee Walker mare figure portrays a mare with graceful proportions and a calm, confident stance. Hand-painted to highlight the musculature and coat details, this figure reflects the quiet strength of a female horse.
marketingDescription: Tennessee Walker horses have unusually long and flat hooves. They are not fitted with horseshoes and are not trimmed. This favours their typical gaits, flat walk (accelerated pace), running walk (running pace) and canter (soft gallop).

name: Hanoverian Gelding
description: This Schleich Hanoverian Gelding figure is a hand-painted replica of the Hanoverian warmblood from northern Germany, showcasing the athletic build and powerful gaits that make this breed a champion in equestrian sport.

name: Pintabian Mare
description: This Schleich Pintabian Mare figure portrays a mare with graceful proportions and a calm, confident stance. Hand-painted to highlight the musculature and coat details, this figure reflects the quiet strength of a female horse.

name: Pintabian Foal
description: This Schleich Pintabian Foal figure depicts a young foal with characteristic long legs and curious posture. Hand-painted with careful attention to detail, the figure captures the playful energy that makes foals so appealing.

name: Pintabian Stallion
description: This Schleich Pintabian Stallion figure depicts a stallion in a powerful, commanding pose. Hand-painted with fine detail to capture the muscular build and spirited character of a male horse.

name: Stegosaurus
description: This Schleich Stegosaurus figure is a meticulously detailed replica of Stegosaurus, with its iconic double row of bony plates and four-spiked tail, one of the most distinctive dinosaurs. A collectible piece in the Schleich prehistoric range.

name: Allosaurus
description: This Schleich Allosaurus figure is a meticulously detailed replica of Allosaurus, capturing the fearsome build and serrated teeth of the dominant Jurassic apex predator. A collectible piece in the Schleich prehistoric range.

name: Brachiosaurus
description: This Schleich Brachiosaurus figure is a meticulously detailed replica of Brachiosaurus, capturing the incredible height and long neck of one of the tallest dinosaurs to walk the Earth. A collectible piece in the Schleich prehistoric range.
marketingDescription: Brachiosaurus translated means "arm lizard". It acquired the name, because its front legs were significantly longer than its hind legs. As such, its long neck pointed automatically upwards and it could eat taller plants with less effort.

name: Utahraptor
description: This Schleich Utahraptor figure is a detailed, hand-painted replica of a prehistoric creature, carefully sculpted to reflect palaeontological understanding with realistic proportions and lifelike detail.
marketingDescription: Utahraptor was a fast, agile raptor, which would hunt herbivorous dinosaurs. On the second toe of its hind foot, it had an unusually long, crescent-shaped claw. Probably it helped it to hold onto prey like Iguanodon.

name: Kentrosaurus
description: This Schleich Kentrosaurus figure is a detailed, hand-painted replica of a prehistoric creature, carefully sculpted to reflect palaeontological understanding with realistic proportions and lifelike detail.

name: Acrocanthosaurus
description: This Schleich Acrocanthosaurus figure is a detailed, hand-painted replica of a prehistoric creature, carefully sculpted to reflect palaeontological understanding with realistic proportions and lifelike detail.
marketingDescription: The Acrocanthosaurus was around the same size and weight as the Tyrannosaurus Rex and Giganotosaurus. Unlike many other dinosaurs, it had seven openings in its skull rather than five. It also had lots of neural spines on its neck, back and tail, which probably formed a small "sail".

name: Mini Tyrannosaurus Rex
description: This Schleich Mini Tyrannosaurus Rex figure is a meticulously detailed replica of the fearsome T. rex, capturing the powerful jaws and menacing stance of one of the largest land predators that ever lived. A collectible piece in the Schleich prehistoric range.

name: Mini Utahraptor
description: This Schleich Mini Utahraptor figure is a detailed, hand-painted replica of a prehistoric creature, carefully sculpted to reflect palaeontological understanding with realistic proportions and lifelike detail.

name: Mini Velociraptor
description: This Schleich Mini Velociraptor figure is a meticulously detailed replica of Velociraptor, featuring the distinctive sickle-shaped claw and alert posture of this intelligent feathered predator. A collectible piece in the Schleich prehistoric range.

name: Mini Spinosaurus
description: This Schleich Mini Spinosaurus figure is a meticulously detailed replica of Spinosaurus, the largest carnivorous dinosaur, featuring the distinctive sail-like back structure and crocodile-like snout. A collectible piece in the Schleich prehistoric range.

name: Asian Elephant Female
description: This Schleich Asian Elephant Female figure captures the wrinkled skin, large ears, and powerful trunk of the world's largest land animal, with the impressive tusks of a bull elephant. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.
marketingDescription: The trunk of an elephant is as versatile as a Swiss army knife. Not only can the elephant smell really well with it, it uses it to suck in water, steady itself, lift trees up high, or spray itself with cool mud. The elephant eats by wrapping its trunk around leaves and grasses, whereby it plucks them from the tree and feeds them into its mouth. A grown animal devours up to 150 kg of green foliage a day. Elephants can even pronounce human-like words with their trunks. To do this, they put their trunks in the mouth and place it on the tongue.

name: Asian Elephant male
description: This Schleich Asian Elephant male figure captures the wrinkled skin, large ears, and powerful trunk of the world's largest land animal, with the impressive tusks of a bull elephant. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.

name: Asian Elephant Calf
description: This Schleich Asian Elephant Calf figure is a hand-painted farm animal replica with realistic proportions and distinctive features.

name: Kangaroo
description: This Schleich Kangaroo figure captures the powerful hind legs, long tail, and upright posture of Australia's most iconic marsupial. Hand-painted with lifelike detail.
marketingDescription: These marsupials have a kind of rubber band in their hind legs. It propels them upwards with each jump. The faster a kangaroo bounces, the farther the animal is "catapulted" forwards, and the less energy it consumes.

name: Jaguar
description: This Schleich Jaguar figure is a hand-painted wildlife replica capturing the natural beauty of this species with lifelike detail.
marketingDescription: Jaguars have the strongest bite of any big cat: twice as strong as that of a lion. With it, jaguars can even crack open tortoise shells without any effort. It's easy to tell jaguars apart from leopards, since they have spots in the middle of the flower-shaped markings on their coats.

name: Gorilla, male
description: This Schleich Gorilla, male figure captures the powerful build and thoughtful expression of the largest living primate, with the distinctive silver back of a dominant male. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.
marketingDescription: Male gorillas often beat their fists on their chests. They do so to show who is the strongest in the group. However, it can also be a kind of greeting. Gorillas don't just walk on the palms of their hands, but also on their knuckles. For this reason, they're also known as knuckle-walkers.

name: Gorilla, Female
description: This Schleich Gorilla, Female figure captures the powerful build and thoughtful expression of the largest living primate, with the distinctive silver back of a dominant male. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.

name: Giant panda, male
description: This hand-painted Schleich Giant panda, male figure captures the distinctive features of this creature with remarkable attention to detail. Part of the Schleich collection since 2017, this figure is crafted to be as realistic as possible, making it perfect for imaginative play and collection.
marketingDescription: Pandas have a striking black and white coat that makes it easier for other pandas to spot them in dense woodland. Scientists are sure this must be the explanation for it, since pandas don't need to worry about camouflaging themselves. They are so large and heavy that they have no natural enemies.

name: Giant Panda, Female
description: This hand-painted Schleich Giant Panda, Female figure captures the distinctive features of this creature with remarkable attention to detail. Part of the Schleich collection since 2017, this figure is crafted to be as realistic as possible, making it perfect for imaginative play and collection.

name: Black panther
description: This Schleich Black panther figure is a hand-painted wildlife replica capturing the natural beauty of this species with lifelike detail.
marketingDescription: Jaguars and leopards with black coats are called black panthers. Despite their black coats, they still have the characteristic flower-shaped markings. Since these markings are as dark as their coat, you can only see them when the light is right.

name: Orangutan Female
description: This Schleich Orangutan Female figure captures the long arms, shaggy coat, and wise expression of the largest tree-dwelling mammal. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.
marketingDescription: Orang-utans are highly intelligent apes who can recognise their own reflection. They can also use tools. For example, they might stick a long branch vertically into a lake to find out how deep it is, or use it to make a bridge over marshy ground before they cross.

name: Orangutan Young
description: This Schleich Orangutan Young figure captures the long arms, shaggy coat, and wise expression of the largest tree-dwelling mammal. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy. This young figure captures the endearing proportions of a juvenile.

name: Toucan
description: This Schleich Toucan figure showcases this Toucan, with its enormous colourful bill — up to a third of its total body length. Hand-painted with realistic colouring and fine detail.

name: Green Anaconda
description: This Schleich Green Anaconda figure is a hand-painted reptile replica with lifelike scales, textures, and natural posture.

name: Sloth Bear
description: This Schleich Sloth Bear figure captures the powerful build and impressive stature of this formidable mammal. Hand-painted with realistic fur texture and colouring.

name: Bald Eagle
description: This Schleich Bald Eagle figure showcases the Bald Eagle, America's national bird, with its distinctive white head and tail against dark brown plumage. Hand-painted with realistic colouring and fine detail.

name: Moose bull
description: This Schleich Moose bull figure is a hand-painted farm animal replica with realistic proportions and distinctive features.
marketingDescription: The elk is Sweden's national animal and is known for its impressive antlers. However, only male elk have them. Elk bulls start growing the antlers at the age of two. They can reach a weight of up to 20 kg. The bulls shed them each year and grow a new set.

name: Fox
description: This Schleich Fox figure captures the bushy tail, pointed ears, and clever expression of one of nature's most resourceful predators. Hand-painted with realistic fur texture and colouring.
marketingDescription: The most famous species of fox is the red fox, which has a characteristic red coat and white chest. Its large, bushy tail helps the small predator keep its balance when running. On cold days, it wraps its tail around its body like a blanket to keep itself warm.

name: Wild Boar
description: This Schleich Wild Boar figure captures the sinuous form and detailed scale pattern of this snake species. Hand-painted with realistic colouring.

name: Sloth
description: This hand-painted Schleich Sloth figure captures the distinctive features of this creature with remarkable attention to detail. Part of the Schleich collection since 2017, this figure is crafted to be as realistic as possible, making it perfect for imaginative play and collection.
marketingDescription: The sloth spends almost its entire life in the trees of tropical rainforests. Its curved claws give it a firm grip on the branches of the trees. Since it always hangs from the branches upside down, the sloth's fur grows from its belly towards its back, instead of growing from its back or head. This allows rainwater to drain away better.

name: Miniature Shetland pony family
description: This Schleich Miniature Shetland pony family figure is a hand-painted replica of the pony breed, capturing the compact build, intelligence, and sure-footedness that make ponies beloved riding partners.
marketingDescription: There's a new addition to the Miniature Shetland Pony family! The newborn foal's ungainliness makes everyone at the stables laugh. When the groom fills a feeding box with crunchy apples, the foal comes right over and curiously investigates it with its muzzle. Meanwhile, the stallion and the mare have started nosing at the bag of feed while nobody was looking. The groom shouldn't have let them out of her sight! The two Miniature Shetland Ponies are already energetically nibbling away to try and get to the feed inside the bags, which smells extremely tempting.

name: Shetland Pony Family
description: This Schleich Shetland Pony Family figure is a hand-painted replica of the pony breed, capturing the compact build, intelligence, and sure-footedness that make ponies beloved riding partners.

name: Spinosaurus and Tyrannosaurus Rex, Small
description: This Schleich Spinosaurus and Tyrannosaurus Rex, Small figure is a meticulously detailed replica of the fearsome T. rex, capturing the powerful jaws and menacing stance of one of the largest land predators that ever lived. A collectible piece in the Schleich prehistoric range.

name: Pegasus
description: This Schleich Pegasus figure depicts the legendary winged horse with magnificent feathered wings. Hand-painted to capture the majesty and freedom of this mythological creature.
marketingDescription: With a single flap of its wings, the flying horse soars up from the ground and quickly vanishes beyond the horizon with a dazzling trail of light. No other being in BAYALA can fly as high and fast as a pegasus. And almost no other creature is as noble or beautiful. If you see one in the sky, it looks like a shooting star. Very few elves have ever been allowed to ride a pegasus. Only those who are chosen can sit on its back and fly from one place to another at incredible speeds.

name: Dragon Ice Hunter
description: This Schleich Dragon Ice Hunter figure is an intricately detailed dragon with powerful wings, sharp claws, and formidable features. Hand-painted to bring the mystery of dragonkind to life.

name: Pegasus Foal
description: This Schleich Pegasus Foal figure depicts a young foal with characteristic long legs and curious posture. Hand-painted with careful attention to detail, the figure captures the playful energy that makes foals so appealing.

name: Safenja's Cloud Squirrel
description: This Schleich Safenja's Cloud Squirrel figure captures the bushy tail, bright eyes, and alert posture of this agile tree-dweller. Hand-painted with realistic fur texture and colouring.

name: Lunaja's Moon Fox
description: This Schleich Lunaja's Moon Fox figure is a beautifully crafted fantasy creature, hand-painted with imaginative detail for creative play and collection.

name: Zenaja's Blossom Deer
description: This Schleich Zenaja's Blossom Deer figure is a beautifully crafted fantasy creature, hand-painted with imaginative detail for creative play and collection.

name: Femaja's Butterfly Foal
description: This Schleich Femaja's Butterfly Foal figure depicts a young foal with characteristic long legs and curious posture. Hand-painted with careful attention to detail, the figure captures the playful energy that makes foals so appealing.

name: Venuja's Pearl Foal
description: This Schleich Venuja's Pearl Foal figure depicts a young foal with characteristic long legs and curious posture. Hand-painted with careful attention to detail, the figure captures the playful energy that makes foals so appealing.

name: Dragon Night Hunter
description: This Schleich Dragon Night Hunter figure is an intricately detailed dragon with powerful wings, sharp claws, and formidable features. Hand-painted to bring the mystery of dragonkind to life.

name: Dragon Poacher
description: This Schleich Dragon Poacher figure is an intricately detailed dragon with powerful wings, sharp claws, and formidable features. Hand-painted to bring the mystery of dragonkind to life.

name: Friesian Stallion
description: This Schleich Friesian Stallion figure is a hand-painted replica of the Friesian breed from the Netherlands, capturing the striking all-black coat, flowing mane and tail, and noble bearing of this elegant breed.

name: Andalusian mare
description: This Schleich Andalusian mare figure is a hand-painted replica of the Andalusian breed, originating from the Iberian Peninsula, showcasing its elegant build and powerful yet graceful stance suited to classical dressage.
marketingDescription: In the 15th century, monks began to breed the Andalusians in the south of Spain. With its graceful appearance, it enchanted especially nobles and kings. Therefore, only few would guess that the Andalusian has long been used as a cavalry horse. Although it was always known for his courage and strength, it is rather their elegance that distinguishes it. It is a unique dressage horse and even dominates the famous leap into the air, which is otherwise known only from the higher riding school of the Lipizzaner horse.

name: Pinto foal
description: This Schleich Pinto foal figure depicts a young foal with characteristic long legs and curious posture. Hand-painted with careful attention to detail, the figure captures the playful energy that makes foals so appealing.
marketingDescription: Pintos are often confused with Paint Horses, because they look very similar. However, Paint Horses are a specific breed, while pintos are defined by the appearance of their coat. This means that, in principle, their beautiful coat patterns can appear in any breed. The horses are then referred to as, for example, Arab pintos or Tinker pintos. There are even Pintaloosas - a mix of pinto and Appaloosa. As a rule of thumb: if you don't know the breed of a piebald horse, you can safely say it's a pinto. That's always correct.

name: Tennessee Walker Foal
description: This Schleich Tennessee Walker Foal figure depicts a young foal with characteristic long legs and curious posture. Hand-painted with careful attention to detail, the figure captures the playful energy that makes foals so appealing.

name: Mustang stallion
description: This Schleich Mustang stallion figure is a hand-painted replica of the American Mustang, the free-roaming horse of the West, capturing the hardy, independent spirit of these resilient horses.
marketingDescription: Mustangs are also called "mestengos". This comes from a Spanish word meaning "wild, stray". They were originally domesticated horses that the Spanish brought to America by ship in the 16th century and that ran away once in America. Since they found plenty of fodder on the fertile pastures and had almost no natural predators except pumas, their numbers rapidly increased. Wild Mustangs still roam the countryside to this day. They are led by a brave herd stallion who protects his herd and is usually the father of all the foals.

name: Mustang Mare
description: This Schleich Mustang Mare figure is a hand-painted replica of the American Mustang, the free-roaming horse of the West, capturing the hardy, independent spirit of these resilient horses.

name: Mustang Foal
description: This Schleich Mustang Foal figure is a hand-painted replica of the American Mustang, the free-roaming horse of the West, capturing the hardy, independent spirit of these resilient horses. As a foal, it captures the long-legged charm and playful energy of a young horse.

name: Clydesdale gelding
description: This Schleich Clydesdale gelding figure is a hand-painted replica of the Clydesdale breed from Scotland, showcasing the impressive feathered legs and gentle giant build of this renowned draught horse.
marketingDescription: Many Clydesdales can be easily identified from their white legs. This hair is also known as "feathering". These horses often have a brown coat, but they can also be black, sorrel or roan. Clydesdales are often used as draught horses. For instance, they may haul tree trunks through terrain that is too difficult for tractors to traverse. They are easy to train and keen learners. But they're also excellent horses for riding. However, because the horses are so big, you need special, extra-large saddles to ride them.

name: Clydesdale mare
description: This Schleich Clydesdale mare figure is a hand-painted replica of the Clydesdale breed from Scotland, showcasing the impressive feathered legs and gentle giant build of this renowned draught horse.
marketingDescription: Shoeing a Clydesdale can be very hard work for a farrier. Their horseshoes are not just extremely large, but also weigh over 2.5 kg. This means they weigh four to five times as much as the shoes of warmbloods or thoroughbreds. Although Clydesdales tend to be stocky and extremely heavy, they can move very elegantly. They are famous for their gait: they lift their beautiful white legs very high and with great force. For this reason, they aren't just used as draught horses, but also for shows and street parades.

name: Clydesdale foal
description: This Schleich Clydesdale foal figure is a hand-painted replica of the Clydesdale breed from Scotland, showcasing the impressive feathered legs and gentle giant build of this renowned draught horse. As a foal, it captures the long-legged charm and playful energy of a young horse.
marketingDescription: Clydesdales grow more slowly than other horse breeds. The foals aren't fully grown until the age of seven or eight, instead of four or five. They then weigh over 1,000 kg - as much as a small car. They are so strong that they can pull objects that are much heavier than themselves. This also means they need a lot of food and water. Every day, they need 20-25 kg of grain with minerals and vitamins, and around 50-60 kg hay. Needless to say, this makes them thirsty - very thirsty, in fact. Clydesdales drink over 100 litres of water per day.

name: Arab Stallion
description: This Schleich Arab Stallion figure depicts a stallion in a powerful, commanding pose. Hand-painted with fine detail to capture the muscular build and spirited character of a male horse.

name: Dilophosaurus
description: This Schleich Dilophosaurus figure is a meticulously detailed replica of Dilophosaurus, featuring the pair of distinctive rounded crests on its skull. A collectible piece in the Schleich prehistoric range.
marketingDescription: After the Compsognathus and the Velociraptor, the Dilophosaurus was one of the fastest prehistoric saurians. Despite its six metre-long body, it weighed only 500 kg and had long, powerful hind legs, with which it could easily reach speeds of up to 38 km/h. It had two striking bone crests on its skull, which also led to it being called the "Double Crested Lizard". They were indeed big and long, but too thin to defend itself with. Scientists believe that they were used to impress rivals.

name: Dimetrodon
description: This Schleich Dimetrodon figure is a meticulously detailed replica of Dimetrodon, with its distinctive sail-like back structure — a Permian creature more closely related to mammals than to dinosaurs. A collectible piece in the Schleich prehistoric range.

name: Barapasaurus
description: This Schleich Barapasaurus figure is a detailed, hand-painted replica of a prehistoric creature, carefully sculpted to reflect palaeontological understanding with realistic proportions and lifelike detail.

name: Dunkleosteus
description: This Schleich Dunkleosteus figure is a detailed, hand-painted replica of a prehistoric creature, carefully sculpted to reflect palaeontological understanding with realistic proportions and lifelike detail.
marketingDescription: The Dunkleosteus lived 130-150 million years before the first dinosaurs. It was a fearless hunter who could snap incredibly fast and ate large sharks, and even its own kind if no other prey was to be found. Fossils of fish, which were only half digested, have often been discovered alongside its skeleton. Scientists therefore believe that the armoured fish was not able to chew very well and swallowed it prey in large pieces. It was named after the palaeontologist David Dunkle. The word "osteos" is Greek for "bone".

name: Herrerasaurus
description: This Schleich Herrerasaurus figure is a detailed, hand-painted replica of a prehistoric creature, carefully sculpted to reflect palaeontological understanding with realistic proportions and lifelike detail.

name: Cheetah, female
description: This Schleich Cheetah, female figure captures the sleek build, black "tear marks," and spotted coat of the fastest land animal on Earth. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.
marketingDescription: Cheetahs are excellent hunters. First of all they approach their prey very carefully until they are within a few metres, then when the time is right, they run at lightning speed. If the fleeing prey suddenly changes direction, the Cheetah can simply turn its body mid-leap. Its flexible spine makes this possible. However, if they have no success after a few hundred metres, they have to give up. The high speed is so energy-sapping, they can only keep it up for a short distance.

name: Cheetah Cub
description: This Schleich Cheetah Cub figure captures the sleek build, black "tear marks," and spotted coat of the fastest land animal on Earth. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy. This young figure captures the endearing proportions of a juvenile.
marketingDescription: Baby cheetahs are very helpless and only open their eyes after two weeks. When their mother is out hunting, they are completely defenceless against hungry lions and hyenas. The best possible camouflage is the only chance they have. A long, silver mane helps them here. It stands up like a mohican hairstyle and looks like long blades of grass from afar. Baby cheetahs learn to hunt for themselves when they are around six months old. After one or two years, the little ones are out of the woods and can fend for themselves.

name: Leopard
description: This Schleich Leopard figure captures the beautiful rosette-patterned coat and stealthy build of this adaptable big cat. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.

name: Giraffe, male
description: This Schleich Giraffe, male figure captures the incredibly long neck, spotted pattern, and graceful proportions of the tallest living animal. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.
marketingDescription: Giraffe bulls are so large, they can put their head through the window of the second floor of a building and say "hello". Their tongue is up to 50 centimetres long, is desensitised to prickly leaves, and has an unusual colour: it is dark blue. This colour helps protect it from sunburn when giraffes use it to wrap around and pluck leaves. Even animals as large as giraffes need to sleep between meals. When there is no threat of danger, they lie down for a nap, but in the wilderness they mainly sleep standing up.

name: Giraffe, female
description: This Schleich Giraffe, female figure captures the incredibly long neck, spotted pattern, and graceful proportions of the tallest living animal. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.
marketingDescription: Despite the camouflage spots in their fur, giraffes do not always remain undetected by predators. But they know to defend themselves and can kick really hard with their front hooves. They mainly do this if they want to protect their young, because the adult animals are normally rarely attacked. Anyone who has the opportunity to look at giraffes from close up can easily distinguish the females from the males. They are somewhat smaller and have thin horns with a small tuft of fur on top. Giraffe bulls, in contrast, have thick horns with no fur on top.

name: Giraffe calf
description: This Schleich Giraffe calf figure is a hand-painted farm animal replica with realistic proportions and distinctive features.
marketingDescription: Baby giraffes are reared together with other young animals in a kind of kindergarten. A female giraffe from the herd acts as the "nursery school teacher" and looks after the calves while the other mothers search for food. If predators approach, she immediately raises the alarm. Then the female giraffes stand protectively in front of their babies and kick at the attackers. At around four months, they start to pluck and eat the small leaves from trees, but they still suckle from their mothers until they are ninth months.

name: Pelican
description: This Schleich Pelican figure showcases this Pelican, with its distinctive throat pouch and broad wingspan. Hand-painted with realistic colouring and fine detail.

name: Giant Pangolin
description: This Schleich Giant Pangolin figure captures the overlapping keratin scales of the world's only truly scaly mammal. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.

name: Flamingo
description: This Schleich Flamingo figure showcases the Flamingo, with its iconic pink plumage, curved neck, and distinctive one-legged stance. Hand-painted with realistic colouring and fine detail.

name: Oryx
description: This hand-painted Schleich Oryx figure captures the distinctive features of this creature with remarkable attention to detail. Part of the Schleich collection since 2016, this figure is crafted to be as realistic as possible, making it perfect for imaginative play and collection.

name: African Reed Frog
description: This Schleich African Reed Frog figure showcases the smooth skin and powerful hind legs of this fascinating amphibian. Hand-painted with lifelike colouring and texture.

name: African elephant, female
description: This Schleich African elephant, female figure captures the wrinkled skin, large ears, and powerful trunk of the world's largest land animal, with the impressive tusks of a bull elephant. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.
marketingDescription: Elephants are known as being thick skinned. However, their skin is so sensitive, they can even get sunburnt. That is why elephants often throw several layers of mud on themselves. This pleasant "cooling pack" also combats the scorching heat. While elephants cannot hear any better than other animals with their huge ears, they help them to cope with the African heat. When they fan with their ears, the blood cools down and provides them with refreshment.

name: African elephant, male
description: This Schleich African elephant, male figure captures the wrinkled skin, large ears, and powerful trunk of the world's largest land animal, with the impressive tusks of a bull elephant. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.
marketingDescription: Elephant bulls are considerably bigger than elephant cows. Reaching heights of almost four metres, they are the second largest land animals after giraffes. They also weigh five to six tonnes, which makes them the heaviest of all land animals. African elephant bulls usually leave their herd when they are about twelve years old. By then they are grown-up enough to find their own food and defend themselves, so they move through the country with other bulls or as a loner - in search of another herd.

name: African elephant calf
description: This Schleich African elephant calf figure is a hand-painted farm animal replica with realistic proportions and distinctive features.
marketingDescription: Baby elephants are still quite clumsy after their birth and must first learn how to handle their trunks. They are still not able to feed themselves with it, so suckle from their mothers for the first year. In the process they gain a good kilo in weight each day. It is very important that they grow big quickly, because a lion or crocodile can hardly cause any harm to healthy, adult animals. If a predator does come dangerously close, the adults form a circle around the young. They are well protected in the middle.

marketingDescription: This family of rainbow unicorns lives on a grassy plain by the rainbow river. In the light of the morning sun, the manes of the three magical creatures gleam in all the colours of the rainbow. Like most unicorns, they are very shy. The handful of lucky individuals who have seen them report that they are incredibly colourful and graceful. And they tell tales of gemstones in all the colours of the rainbow that adorn the flanks of the magical unicorns. Many mysterious tales are told about these creatures - and nobody knows whether the tales are true. But all the tales agree that the rainbow unicorns are incredibly beautiful and magical creatures.

name: Tinker mare
description: This Schleich Tinker mare figure is a hand-painted replica of the Tinker (Irish Cob), showcasing the sturdy, compact build, abundant feathering, and striking piebald or skewbald colouring of this traditional breed.
marketingDescription: In the Tinker are the mighty Shire horse, the powerful Clydesdale, the majestic Friesian and the sure-footed Dales Pony. These were bred by travellers to the UK and Ireland to make for a strong and sturdy pull- and workhorse. At the same time they stayed very much on top of the horses characters. They should be absolutely gentle and reliable. To date, the Tinker is known and loved for these characteristics. It is therefore often used as a therapy horse.

marketingDescription: Pintos have unique, beautiful fur drawings that give the horses more personality. Nevertheless, the Pintos do not form a separate breed. They are a colour breed, which is found in almost all breeds. The coat patterns are different in "Overo" and "Tobiano". Overos have a coloured coat with white piebald. Their head is often completely white. It's a so-called "lantern". Tobianos on the other hand, have a white coat with coloured piebald. Legs and back remain mostly white.

name: Lion, roaring
description: This Schleich Lion, roaring figure captures the powerful build and commanding presence of the "King of the Jungle". Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.
marketingDescription: The larger the mane of a lion, the more frightening it is for intruders of its territory, which it defends. Lions are the only ones who hunt together in prides - although this task is usually left to the lionesses. Since they are not very fast, unlike the cheetah, they use their strength as a pride. They prowl from different directions, circle their prey and use the element of surprise. That way they can kill large ungulates such as wildebeest and zebras and even buffalos and weak elephants.

name: Alligator
description: This Schleich Alligator figure showcases the broad snout, armoured hide, and powerful build of this impressive semi-aquatic reptile. Hand-painted with lifelike colouring and texture.
marketingDescription: The alligator is a dangerous predator with exceptionally strong jaw muscles. Only the white shark can bite harder than him. The interesting thing: Only the muscles that close the jaws are extremely strong. The muscles that open the snout are so weak that they can be closed with simple tape. Compared to the saltwater crocodile, alligators are smaller and much wider. They live only in rivers and lakes in the south-eastern part of the United States and China.

name: Alligator Baby
description: This Schleich Alligator Baby figure showcases the broad snout, armoured hide, and powerful build of this impressive semi-aquatic reptile. Hand-painted with lifelike colouring and texture.

name: Tiger
description: This Schleich Tiger figure captures the striking orange-and-black striped coat and intense gaze of the largest wild cat. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.
marketingDescription:
Like all tigers, this one prowls on thick, padded feet...so he can sneak a cookie from the snack jar before dinner. A natural-born predator, he fears only two things: Puppies and the vacuum. Enjoys long soaks in a bubble bath.

schleich® wild animal figurines for toddlers and kids introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play.  From realistic ocean and jungle animal figurines to our popular playsets, schleich® WILD LIFE lets your stories run wild. Authentic wild creature figurines toys prowl, swim, and soar with rich, accurate detail and texture, while our full-featured wild life playsets bring learning to life. Our wild animal toys love cross into new play worlds to create stories with schleich® enchanted creature toys, farm animal figurines and dinosaur toys! schleich® wild animal toys and playsets are built to last for generations. All our toys meet the highest national and international safety standards.

name: Tiger cub
description: This Schleich Tiger cub figure captures the striking orange-and-black striped coat and intense gaze of the largest wild cat. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy. This young figure captures the endearing proportions of a juvenile.
marketingDescription: The tiger's stripes are as varied and unique as fingerprints. Even the skin underneath is striped. At first glance, the striking pattern hardly seems to be beneficial. After all, the tiger does not want to be discovered by its prey immediately. When the tiger goes hunting in the twilight, it is however perfectly camouflaged with the stripes. They appear as tall grass moving in the wind or like fleeting shadows. Unlike many other "cats" and big cats the tiger swims well and likes it, too. Therefore, the water is not a safe place for many prey animals.

name: Tiger, white
description: This Schleich Tiger, white figure captures the striking orange-and-black striped coat and intense gaze of the largest wild cat. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.
marketingDescription: White tigers are breathtakingly beautiful. They have a snow-white fur and strong, bright blue eyes. They differ from normal tigers because fur, skin and eyes cannot form red and yellow pigments. However, they are not albinos, for those always have red eyes. White tigers usually live in zoos because in the wild they would have little chance of survival. Because their fur is missing the camouflage, prey would discover them quickly and run away early enough.

name: Tiger cub, white
description: This Schleich Tiger cub, white figure captures the striking orange-and-black striped coat and intense gaze of the largest wild cat. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy. This young figure captures the endearing proportions of a juvenile.
marketingDescription: White tigers are breathtakingly beautiful. They have a snow-white fur and strong, bright blue eyes. They differ from normal tigers because fur, skin and eyes cannot form red and yellow pigments. However, they are not albinos, for those always have red eyes. White tigers usually live in zoos because in the wild they would have little chance of survival. Because their fur is missing the camouflage, prey would discover them quickly and run away early enough.

name: Cobra
description: This Schleich Cobra figure showcases the distinctive spread hood and alert upright posture of this iconic venomous snake. Hand-painted with lifelike colouring and texture.

name: Giant Panda Cub - playing
description: This hand-painted Schleich Giant Panda Cub - playing figure captures the distinctive features of this creature with remarkable attention to detail. Part of the Schleich collection since 2015, this figure is crafted to be as realistic as possible, making it perfect for imaginative play and collection.
marketingDescription: Panda bears start small. Extremely small. At birth they weigh just around 100 grams. That's as much as a bar of chocolate or a small yogurt. Apart from the Kangaroos, no other mammal gives birth to such small babies. After four weeks, the cubs have their typical black and white fur, after 6-8 weeks, they open their eyes for the first time. They remain with their mother for about two years and are very playful. Their mother likes to roll them back and forth gently and lovingly grapples around with them.

name: Hyena
description: This Schleich Hyena figure captures the powerful jaws and spotted coat of this highly successful African predator. Hand-painted with lifelike accuracy.

name: Crocodile
description: This Schleich Crocodile figure showcases the powerful jaws and armoured body of one of nature's most ancient predators, unchanged for 200 million years. Hand-painted with lifelike colouring and texture.
marketingDescription: The saltwater crocodile is the most dangerous of all crocodiles. It holds the record for the largest crocodile: It can weigh up to a ton, as much as a small car. While most crocodiles live in rivers and lakes, the saltwater crocodile is the only one that can deal with both fresh water and saltwater of the seas. In addition to the saltwater crocodile, there are many other kinds, a total of 25. These include, for example, the Nile crocodile, the armoured crocodile, alligators and caimans.

name: Macaw
description: This Schleich Macaw figure showcases this Macaw, with vibrant multi-coloured plumage and powerful curved beak of the largest parrot species. Hand-painted with realistic colouring and fine detail.
marketingDescription: The Scarlet Macaw is known for its magnificent plumage that glows in bright colours. It is not only beautiful but it is also very noticeable. In its home, the rain forest, however, he just blends in with the green leaves and colourful fruits. Its favourite foods include not only fruits but also Brazil nuts, of which it breaks the hard shell easily with its beak. You can often see Scarlet Macaws how they lick clay on riverbanks. The clay renders toxins in unripe fruit harmless and for the macaw it works as a home remedy for stomach aches.

name: Polar bear cub, walking
description: This Schleich Polar bear cub, walking figure captures the powerful build and impressive stature of this formidable mammal. Hand-painted with realistic fur texture and colouring. This young figure captures the curious nature of a juvenile.
marketingDescription: The polar and grizzly bears are the largest animal predators on Earth. Weighing more than 500 kilos with a length of almost 3 meters, this bear is double the size of a Siberian tiger. Polar bears have a thick layer of fat in order to be able to defy the extreme Arctic cold. They also have an undercoat and a layer of transparent protective hair. As a result they can swim and hunt in very low temperatures. Polar bears are very good swimmers and have even been seen 300 kilometers from the coast. When searching for food the polar bear has the benefit of a highly sensitive sense of smell. They can perceive a smell that is one and a half kilometers away or buried one metre under the snow! Their hearing and sight are comparable to that of a human. Polar bears are loners, but they have no fixed territory and enjoy playing with each other. The mating season is in May and during gestation the female polar bear almost triples her weight.

description: This Schleich White Tailed Deer, Female figure captures the graceful proportions and alert ears of this elegant woodland herbivore. Hand-painted with realistic fur texture and colouring.
marketingDescription: Weißwedelhirsche grasen morgens und abends. Als Pflanzenfresser ernähren sie sich von grünen Pflanzen und Nüssen. Hirsche leben in kleinen Gruppen -- meist nur eine Mutter und ihre Kälber. Die Männchen haben herrliche Geweihe und leben in kleinen Gruppen von drei oder vier Tieren. Sowohl männliche Hirsche als auch Hirschkühe haben im Sommer rotbraunes und im Winter graubraunes Fell. Da sie kaum noch natürliche Feinde haben, wachsen Hirschpopulationen manchmal so stark an, dass sie nicht mehr genügend Nahrung finden. Hirsche sind Wiederkäuer wie Rinder. Das bedeutet, dass sie vier Mägen haben und halb verdaute Nahrung dann wiederkauen. Dieser komplizierte Vorgang ermöglicht es Hirschen, beispielsweise Zweige zu fressen, die die meisten Tiere nicht verdauen können.

marketingDescription: Thanks to their black and white feathers, the orange patterned beak and their bright red feet, puffins are beautiful birds. For the most part they live and breed in cold, polar regions. Outside of the breeding season they are out at sea. Their hunting technique is particularly amazing. They dive below the sea's surface, following the fish by paddling with their wings to propel them forwards.  In doing so, they can dive over 50 meters deep. However they usually catch the fish in shallower depths.

marketingDescription: Grizzlies are tall, strong animals - they stand up to 2.4 metres high and weigh roundabout 450 kilograms. They have sort of a hump on their back and strong hind limbs. Grizzly bears are able to run up to 55 kilometres per hour. They are loners and frequently behave aggressively. Sometimes they gather at waters to catch salmon. marketingDescription: Although the Tyrannosaurus Rex looked quite threatening, it could hardly use its front legs armed with just two claws since they did not even reach his mouth. Nearly seven metres tall and thirteen metres long, the Tyrannosaurus Rex was probably not a very skilled hunter, but rather a scavenger which probably fed on the leavings of other animals. It had more than fifty teeth measuring more than 15 centimetres in length which it used not for chewing but rather for tearing, since it swallowed its food in large chunks. To improve depth-perception when searching for food, the eyes of the Tyrannosaurus Rex were oriented toward the front such that the fields of vision of both eyes overlapped.
